items.q: What state are the ions in when defining lattice enthalpy of formation?
items.correct: Gaseous ions — lattice enthalpy is the energy change when one mole of solid ionic compound forms from its constituent ions in the GAS phase.
items.misconception: The ions are in aqueous solution before forming the lattice.
items.random_wrong: The ions start as solid elements before being assembled into the lattice.
items.why: Students confuse lattice enthalpy with enthalpy of solution; they don't realise the Born-Haber cycle requires gas-phase ions as the reference point.

items.q: Why can an endothermic reaction still be spontaneous?
items.correct: If the entropy increase (TΔS) is large enough to outweigh the positive ΔH, ΔG can still be negative.
items.misconception: Endothermic reactions are never spontaneous because they require energy input.
items.random_wrong: All endothermic reactions are spontaneous at room temperature.
items.why: Students equate 'exothermic' with 'spontaneous' from GCSE, missing the role of entropy in Gibbs free energy.

items.q: Why is the hydration enthalpy of a small, highly charged ion very exothermic?
items.correct: Small/highly charged ions form strong ion-dipole attractions with water molecules, releasing a large amount of energy.
items.misconception: Hydration enthalpy only depends on the size of the ion, not its charge.
items.random_wrong: Hydration enthalpy is the same for all ions because water molecules are identical.
items.why: Students focus on one factor (size OR charge) when actually both matter — charge density determines the strength of ion-dipole interactions.

items.q: Sign of first electron affinity for Cl?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Negative. “Positive” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Exothermic. “Endothermic” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: ΔHf=-411, atomise Na=108, 1IE=496, atomise Cl=122, EA Cl=-349. LE (kJ/mol)?
items.why: Exact answer: -788. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
